title: 10 best herbal creams for oily skin

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with oily skin because they contain natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to regulate oil production and reduce acne-causing bacteria. The tea tree extract in these creams works to unclog pores, reducing the appearance of blackheads and whiteheads, while also controlling inflammation and redness associated with oily skin. This results in a more balanced complexion, reduced shine, and an overall improvement in skin health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tea tree oil's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema.
Antifungal properties: Tea tree oil's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can cause skin issues like dandruff, psoriasis, and ringworm, which can exacerbate oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in terpenes and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to help control acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
Buds: Containing a high concentration of cineole, a compound that helps to regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin, making it effective in treating acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves from healthy plants in the morning.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 4 hours.
Infuse 20g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 200ml of sweet almond oil overnight.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and filter with a coffee filter to separate solids from liquids.
Mix the filtered liquid with 30% beeswax, 15% coconut oil, and 1% vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with oily skin because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's soothing and calming effect reduces redness and irritation caused by excessive oil production, allowing the pores to breathe and reduce congestion. Additionally, lavender's astringent qualities help to shrink the appearance of pores, leaving the skin looking smoother and more refined.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing clogged pores, which can contribute to oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin, while also helping to regulate sebum production.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and accelerate aging, which can lead to oily skin; they also help to stabilize skin cell membranes, improving skin elasticity and texture.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming oily skin.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, which help to balance and regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in their peak blooming season.
Prepare the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers by grinding them into a fine powder.
Combine the ground lavandula angustifolia powder with sweet almond oil and beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl until melted and well combined.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with oily skin because they have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. The gel-like consistency of aloe vera also helps to regulate oil production by penetrating deep into the pores, gently cleansing and purifying the skin without stripping it of its natural moisture. This leaves the skin looking clearer, more balanced, and less prone to shine and greasiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, while also calming and soothing the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal aloe cream's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can cause damage to the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, and protecting the skin from further environmental stressors.

Natural antibacterial properties: Aloe vera's natural antibacterial properties help to prevent bacterial growth and infection, which is especially important for oily skin prone to clogged pores and acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing high concentrations of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and minerals, aloe leaves help to soothe and calm oily skin while reducing inflammation.
Stems: The stems of the aloe plant are rich in antioxidants and have natural anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in controlling sebum production and reducing redness in oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and slice them into thin pieces for immediate use.
Combine one cup of sliced aloe vera gel with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a blender.
Add five drops of tea tree essential oil and three drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture.
Stir well and pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars before refrigerating for 24 hours.
After 24 hours, stir the mixture again and use it within two weeks or store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with oily skin because they harness the natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of herbs to balance and regulate oil production. Herbal tea ingredients such as green tea, chamomile, and licorice root help to calm and soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and redness caused by excess oil. Additionally, these herbal teas contain antioxidants that work to neutralize free radicals, further controlling sebum production and leaving the skin looking clearer and more refined.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas like green tea, chamomile, and lavender contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the skin, which can contribute to oiliness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Teas like peppermint, ginger, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin.

Antibacterial properties: Herbal teas like tea tree, chamomile, and calendula contain antibacterial agents that can help control acne-causing bacteria and other microorganisms on the skin, reducing oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots help regulate oil production and reduce inflammation in the skin.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, barks have astringent properties that help tighten pores and control sebum secretion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh green tea leaves and dry them completely using air circulation for at least 2 weeks.
Grind 20g of dried green tea leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered green tea with 100g of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Add 10g of beeswax, 5g of coconut oil, and 5g of shea butter to the infused green tea liquid and melt them together in a double boiler over low heat.
Whip the melted mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and becomes smooth, taking about 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants in ginger help to reduce sebum production, which can clog pores and contribute to acne-prone skin. Additionally, ginger's ability to balance the skin's pH levels can help to regulate oil production, leaving skin feeling smooth and refreshed. By reducing inflammation and redness, herbal ginger creams can also improve skin tone and texture, giving users a more radiant and healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Ginger's antibacterial compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can clog pores and contribute to acne and oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and paradol, help to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can exacerbate conditions like acne, rosacea, and oily skin by increasing redness and swelling.
Astringent properties: Ginger's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, leaving skin looking cleaner and more balanced.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of the ginger plant, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce acne and soothe irritated skin.
Roots: Ginger roots are used to create creams that help to balance the skin's natural pH level, reducing excess oil production and clarifying the complexion.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20 grams of raw ginger root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered ginger with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container and steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the ginger infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids and squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax pellets with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler until fully incorporated and melted for 10 minutes.

Combine the ginger infusion with the beeswax mixture and whip using an electric mixer until smooth and creamy for 2 minutes, then pour into sterilized jars and allow to cool at room temperature before storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with oily skin because they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce sebum production and soothe the skin. The herb's natural astringent properties also help to tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oiliness and preventing breakouts. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compound, calendic acid, helps reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective treatment for reducing inflammation in oily skin.

Antibacterial property: The cream's antibacterial properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), help combat bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to clogged pores and excess oil production in oily skin.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and lead to signs of aging, such as fine lines and wrinkles, making it an effective treatment for mature or damaged skin prone to oiliness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain a high amount of omega-3 fatty acids, which help to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of the calendula powder with 20 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add 5 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated, allowing it to cool for 15 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler or a heat-proof glass measuring cup set over simmering water, heating it to 160°f to 180°f for 30-40 minutes.
Remove from heat, let it cool slightly, and whip the cream with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with oily skin because they contain a natural compound that blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to excess oil production. By regulating sebum secretion, saw palmetto cream reduces the appearance of pores and minimizes shine. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help calm redness and irritation often associated with oily skin. As a result, saw palmetto creams provide a gentle and effective way to control oiliness and achieve a balanced, healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can lead to excessive oil production in the skin.

Sebum-regulating activity: Saw palmetto's beta-sitosterol content has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can stimulate sebaceous glands and lead to oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne and oily skin, allowing for clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saw palmetto roots contain a unique fatty acid called beta-sitosterol, which has been shown to reduce sebum production and help regulate oil levels in the skin.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant are rich in antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids, which can help to soothe and calm oily skin, reducing inflammation and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of serenoa repens and dry them in the shade for at least 7 days to preserve potency.
Grind dried serenoa repens leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of ground serenoa repens with 100 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 30 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 60 minutes before transferring it to glass jars with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent properties, which help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production. The soothing and calming effects of chamomile also help to calm inflammation and redness associated with acne-prone skin. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce the appearance of large pores, giving the skin a more even and refined texture. This results in a balanced complexion that is less oily and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, which can exacerbate oily skin, promoting a calm and balanced complexion.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant properties combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, helping to regulate sebum production and maintain healthy skin tone.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects help reduce irritation and discomfort caused by oily skin, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving itchiness and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation, making them effective for controlling oiliness.
Seeds: Containing a unique compound called matricin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers at dawn or dusk to ensure optimal potency then sift through fine mesh.
Combine one part dried chamomile flowers with two parts sweet almond oil in a small heatproof glass bowl.
Allow the mixture to steep in a double boiler for 20-30 minutes or until it reaches a comfortable temperature.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup then discard solids.
Whip heavy coconut cream with one tablespoon of honey and two tablespoons of strained chamomile oil mixture until light and fluffy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with oily skin because of its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to reduce sebum production and combat acne-causing bacteria. The essential oils in rosemary also have astringent and toning effects, tightening pores and reducing inflammation. By balancing the skin's pH levels, rosemary cream can help regulate oil production, leaving skin looking healthier and more radiant with a natural glow.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne, thereby helping to control oil production and reduce sebum buildup.

Astringent properties: Rosemary's astringent properties help to tighten the skin and constrict pores, reducing their size and minimizing the appearance of large pores that are often associated with oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation caused by excess oil production or clogged pores, which can lead to a more balanced and healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation in oily skin.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, seeds provide moisturizing properties while controlling excess oil secretion, making them suitable for balancing oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of rosmarinus officinalis and chop them coarsely into small pieces weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine chopped rosmarinus officinalis with 50 milliliters of distilled water in a heatproof glass measuring cup to create a decoction mixture.
Heat the decoction mixture in a double boiler or steam bath for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to extract the herbal active compounds.
Strain the hot decoction mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Mix the reserved liquid with 50 grams of beeswax and 25 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heatproof double boiler allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with oily skin because they contain a unique combination of herbal ingredients that work synergistically to balance the skin's natural oil production. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of the Tasmanian blue gum help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excess oil. Additionally, the cream's astringent qualities help to tighten pores and reduce shine, leaving the skin looking smooth and refined. As a result, oily skin becomes more manageable and balanced, giving users a healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antibacterial compounds, such as eucalyptol and camphor, help reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, preventing clogged pores and breakouts associated with oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum, like globoid and eugenol, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation that can exacerbate oily skin conditions.

Astringent properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's astringent compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, controlling oiliness and leaving the skin feeling clean and refreshed.
remedy_parts: Barks: Contain tannins, which help to absorb excess oil and reduce inflammation on the skin.
Roots: Rich in saponins, which have natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help control sebum production and soothe acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Extract 10g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 100ml of boiling distilled water for 15 minutes.
Filter the extract through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and discard the residue.
Mix the filtered extract with 50g of sweet almond oil, 20g of beeswax, and 10g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 60°c to 70°c in 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool and thicken at room temperature for about 30 minutes before whipping into a creamy consistency with an electric mixer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. The menthol in peppermint cools the skin, reducing inflammation and excess oil production, leaving pores unclogged and refined. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties help to calm redness and irritation associated with acne-prone skin. As a result, herbal peppermint creams provide a gentle yet effective way to balance and purify oily skin, promoting a healthy and radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Cooling and Astringent Properties: Peppermint's cooling effect helps to reduce inflammation and constrict pores, while its astringent properties help to dry out excess oil and tighten the skin, making it an effective treatment for reducing oiliness.

Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Peppermint's antimicrobial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria and fungi, which can contribute to oily skin by reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Toning and Balancing Properties: Peppermint's toning effect helps to balance the skin's natural pH, reduce sebum production, and regulate oil flow, resulting in a more even-toned and balanced complexion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm oily skin while reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: As a concentrated source of menthol and other essential oils, peppermint rhyzomes provide a potent cooling effect to reduce oiliness and purify the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Blend 50 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of boiling water to create a steeping mixture.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes allowing the menthol and flavonoids to infuse into the water.
Combine the cooled infusion with 200 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax, gently heating the mixture until the wax is fully melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify before use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium creams helps with oily skin because they contain natural astringent properties that help to balance and regulate sebum production. The herbal extracts in geranium creams work to reduce the amount of oil produced by the skin, leaving it looking clearer and less shiny. Additionally, geranium creams can help to tighten pores and reduce inflammation, making them an effective treatment for oily skin prone to acne and blackheads. This natural approach can provide long-term benefits for a healthier, more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Geranium oil has antibacterial properties that help control the growth of bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing clogged pores, which can contribute to oily skin.

Astringent properties: The astringent properties of geranium oil help to tighten pores and reduce their size, allowing for a more even and balanced tone, and reducing the appearance of large pores that can trap excess oil and contribute to oily skin.

Balancing blood flow: Geranium oil has been shown to help balance blood flow and circulation, which can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation, leading to healthier and less oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, geranium roots help balance sebum production and reduce acne-causing bacteria on oily skin.
Leaves: The leaves' astringent properties help tighten pores, reducing the appearance of large pores and oiliness on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind the dried pelargonium graveolens leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground pelargonium graveolens with beeswax and sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3:6 by weight and heat until melted in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add jojoba oil and glycerin to the mixture at a ratio of 2:1 by volume and stir well for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with oily skin because they have natural antiseptic and astringent properties that help to control sebum production. The antimicrobial compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce the amount of excess oil produced by the skin, leaving it feeling balanced and refreshed. Additionally, thyme's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with oily skin conditions. This natural approach can help to achieve a smoother, more even-toned complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, help reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to acne and other inflammatory skin conditions associated with oily skin.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids, help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by oily skin, making it an effective natural treatment for soothing and calming the skin.
Astringent Properties: Thyme's astringent properties help tighten pores, reducing their appearance and minimizing the production of sebum, which can contribute to oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne and excess oil production.
Buds: Thyme buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a reputable source.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 50 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the filtered oil.
Mix one part of the filtered thymus vulgaris oil with three parts of beeswax and heat until melted then pour into sterile cream jars cooling to form a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with oily skin because they possess antiseptic and astringent properties that work to control excess oil production. The herbal extracts in yarrow creams help to reduce sebum secretion, thereby minimizing the appearance of pores and leaving skin looking smoother and more refined. Additionally, yarrow's ability to absorb excess moisture makes it an effective treatment for acne-prone skin, helping to dry up pimples and prevent future breakouts.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in yarrow cream help to reduce sebum production by constricting pores and drying up excess oil, reducing shine and preventing clogged pores.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in yarrow have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm redness and irritation associated with oily skin, while also fighting against free radicals that can cause premature aging.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in yarrow cream, such as camphor and borneol, have antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help to control acne-causing bacteria, reducing breakouts and preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of saponins, which help to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce excess oil production.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain acacetin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe and calm oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh acmilea millefolium leaves and stems, clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine one part dried achillea millefolium herb with two parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f for 4-6 hours to extract beneficial properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and measuring final volume.
Mix 10% of the herbal infusion with beeswax and vitamin e oil, melt at 120°f for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with oily skin because of its unique properties that work to balance the skin's natural oil production. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of lemongrass help to reduce inflammation and purify the pores, which in turn reduces excess sebum secretion. Additionally, lemongrass has astringent properties that help to tighten the pores, making it an effective remedy for controlling oily skin. By using lemongrass creams, individuals with oily skin can achieve a more balanced and healthy complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass oil has antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin's surface, which can contribute to acne and other skin issues associated with oily skin.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: The astringent and antiseptic properties of lemongrass oil help to tighten pores and reduce inflammation, reducing the appearance of pores and leaving skin looking smoother and more refined.

Antifungal and decongestant properties: Lemongrass oil's antifungal properties can help to combat fungal infections that can cause skin issues like acne and redness, while its decongestant properties can help to reduce puffiness and tighten the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their ability to balance oil production and reduce inflammation in the skin, making them effective in treating acne-prone skin.
Stems: The stems of lemongrass contain a high concentration of antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that help to purify the skin and control sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and dry them in the shade for four hours to ensure optimal potency.
Grind dried lemongrass into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and mix with 20g of coconut oil.
Combine the lemongrass powder mixture with 10ml of distilled water and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Add 5g of beeswax and 2g of shea butter to the paste and heat it in a double boiler for 30 minutes to melt the waxes.
Pour the melted cream into sterilized containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for three hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with oily skin because they are rich in salicin, a beta hydroxy acid that deep cleans pores and reduces excess oil production. The cream's natural extracts also help to balance the skin's pH, controlling sebum production and preventing clogged pores. Additionally, maidenhair tree contains antioxidants that soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with oily skin. As a result, using maidenhair tree creams can leave your skin looking smoother, clearer, and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal maidenhair tree creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress on the skin, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions.

Soothing and calming properties: The creams' ability to soothe and calm the skin can help reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin, allowing it to regulate its natural oil production and appear more balanced.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal maidenhair tree creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can cause pores to enlarge and lead to excess oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to control sebum production and reduce inflammation in oily skin.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin while reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass container until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 20 drops of jojoba oil, 10 drops of tea tree oil, and 5 grams of kaolin clay to the mixture stirring thoroughly for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture at low temperature (30°c) for 1 hour while stirring occasionally to facilitate infusion of the herbal extract.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with oily skin because of its unique ability to balance the skin's natural oil production. The herbal extract contained in these creams, Echinacea pallida, has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores. Additionally, Kansas coneflower cream's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce their appearance, leaving the skin looking smooth and refined. This natural formula makes it an effective treatment for oily skin, reducing shine and excess oil while maintaining healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower cream help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and preventing damage to the skin caused by excess oil production.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Kansas coneflower cream reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin, helping to soothe and calm the skin without drying it out.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial agents in Kansas coneflower cream help control bacteria that can contribute to acne and oily skin, preventing breakouts and reducing the appearance of pores.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help regulate sebum production and reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin.
Fruits: Containing a high concentration of fatty acids, fruits provide moisturizing properties that soothe and calm the skin without clogging pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia flowers at 20 grams and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground echinacea powder with sweet almond oil at 30 milliliters and beeswax at 5 grams in a double boiler setup for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture under low heat until the beeswax is fully melted, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes total preparation time.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 70°c before whipping with an electric whisk for 10 minutes to emulsify the ingredients.
Pour the whipped cream into a sterilized glass jar, allowing it to cool completely at room temperature or in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with oily skin because of its natural ability to balance sebum production. The antifungal and antibacterial properties in sage cream help to reduce inflammation and combat acne-causing bacteria, making it an effective treatment for clogged pores and excess oil buildup. Additionally, the astringent properties of sage help to tighten pores, reducing their appearance and minimizing the shine associated with oily skin. This results in a more refined, healthier-looking complexion that is less prone to oiliness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions like acne.
Astringent properties: The tannins in sage have an astringent effect, helping to reduce sebum production and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling oiliness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help to reduce inflammation and soothe acne-prone skin.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, sage rhyzomes have natural astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and balance oily skin.
Barks: Sage barks contain tannins and phenolic acids that can help to tighten pores, reduce redness, and prevent bacterial growth on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of salvia officinalis, ensure cleanliness and air-dry them for 2 weeks.
Combine dried salvia leaves with sweet almond oil in a 10-15% ratio by weight for 4 hours.
Infuse the mixture at room temperature, shaking occasionally, for 6 days and then strain it.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 30-40% ratio by weight for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture gently under low heat until beeswax is fully melted and emulsified.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with oily skin because of its natural ability to balance oil production and reduce sebum secretion. The herbal extract contains compounds that target the sebaceous glands, regulating their activity and preventing excess oil from accumulating on the surface of the skin. This results in a smoother, more even-toned complexion that appears less greasy and shiny, while also reducing the appearance of pores. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, making it an effective natural solution for managing oily skin concerns.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Fennel's antibacterial compounds, such as anethole, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin that can contribute to clogged pores and acne.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin and acne.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the skin and contribute to oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing sebum production and soothing acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Known for their natural astringent and antiseptic properties, helping to balance oil production and prevent clogged pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried fennel seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground fennel powder with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for 15 minutes.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 10 drops of lavender essential oil into the fennel-coconut oil mixture until the wax is fully dissolved for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 120°f before whipping it with an electric mixer or whisk for 2 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized jars or tubes, seal them tightly, and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or until opened.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for oily skin are specially formulated products that combine natural ingredients derived from plants, herbs, and botanicals to soothe, calm, and balance the skin. These creams help to reduce excess oil production, purify pores, and regulate sebum levels, making them ideal for individuals with oily complexions. Examples of herbal creams that can benefit oily skin include tea tree oil cream, aloe vera gel, and lavender extract lotion. By using these products, people with oily skin can enjoy clearer, more radiant complexion, reduced acne and inflammation, and a sense of confidence and self-assurance that comes with having healthy-looking skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily skin with herbal creams is to look for products that contain ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and witch hazel. These natural ingredients help to reduce excess oil production, calm acne-prone skin, and soothe inflammation. Apply the cream gently to your face, avoiding the eye area, and massage it in until fully absorbed. For best results, use these creams consistently as part of your daily skincare routine.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal creams are acne, rosacea, and eczema. These conditions often exhibit symptoms such as inflammation, redness, and irritation, which herbal creams can help soothe and calm. Herbal ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile are commonly used in these creams to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing.
